channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Tanzanian Content Creators Adapt and Thrive in the Digital Era
item.description: In Tanzania, many digital creators face a reality far different from the glamour often shown online. Growth is rarely instant; it demands patience, resilience, and constant reinvention. Kiredio, whose...

item.title: Police respond to anti-social behaviour as youths cause disruption in Clapham high street
item.description: On Tuesday afternoon, Clapham high street in south London was once again the scene of disorder as groups of teenagers, influenced by a TikTok trend, congregated in large numbers. Families reportedly h...

item.title: Understanding the Vital Role of Prosecutors in Criminal Cases
item.description: The Rappler Newsbreak episode titled 'Order in the Court: Are you curious what prosecutors do?' explains the key role prosecutors play within the Philippine justice system. Prosecutors serve as the fi...

item.title: Interior Ministry orders Nadra to trace and remove illegally registered foreign citizens
item.description: On March 31, 2026, Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visited the Nadra headquarters in Islamabad and directed the National Database and Registration Authority to immediately identify and take action agai...

item.title: Election tribunal upholds Attaullah Tarar’s victory from NA-127
item.description: LAHORE: The election tribunal on Tuesday has officially dismissed the petition challenging Information Minister Attaullah Tarar’s win from NA-127 in the 2024 general elections, terming it non-maintain...

item.title: Violence Erupts in Eastern Cape Over Alleged Nigerian King Installation
item.description: Violence broke out in KuGompo City, Eastern Cape, South Africa, following the alleged coronation of Nigerian national Solomon Eziko as 'Igwe Ndigbo'. Videos of the ceremony circulated widely, sparking...

item.title: Israel passes death penalty bill for Palestinians amid international criticism
item.description: The United States has stated that it respects Israel’s sovereign right to legislate its own laws after the Israeli parliament passed a bill introducing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of ...

item.title: Glasgow man ordered to repay £46,000 after £1m cocaine pursuit conviction
item.description: A personal trainer from Glasgow, William Paterson, aged 37, has been ordered by the High Court in Edinburgh to repay £46,370 under the Proceeds of Crime Act, following his conviction for drug traffick...

item.title: Upcoming Film Releases Highlight Diverse Range of Content Across Streaming Platforms
item.description: A comprehensive overview of upcoming film releases across various streaming platforms has been compiled, showcasing the diverse range of content available to audiences. The article details new additio...

item.title: Delhi Records Its Wettest March in Three Years with More Rain and Thunderstorms
item.description: The national capital, Delhi, experienced another spell of rain and thunderstorms as March came to an end, marking the city’s wettest March in the past three years. The India Meteorological Department ...

item.title: Rising food prices create Easter grocery budget concerns in Canada
item.description: As Easter approaches, Canadian shoppers are facing higher grocery bills, reflecting a broader trend of rising food prices nationwide. The increase affects traditional seasonal items, making holiday me...

item.title: Keith Urban focusing on music and wellbeing after divorce from Nicole Kidman
item.description: Keith Urban is reportedly in good spirits following his divorce from Nicole Kidman in 2025, after nearly 20 years of marriage. According to friend and fellow musician Russell Dickerson, Urban has been...

item.title: Mysuru man arrested for sending over 1,100 fake bomb threat emails to courts and government offices
item.description: According to the Delhi Police, a 46-year-old man from Mysuru, identified as Srinivas Louis, was arrested for allegedly sending more than 1,100 hoax bomb threat emails to the Supreme Court, various Hig...

item.title: Nairobi Governor Sakaja Meets Senate Clerk After Police Raid City Hall Over Contempt Case
item.description: On Tuesday, Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja went to visit Senate Clerk Jeremiah Nyegenye shortly after police officers stormed City Hall in an effort to arrest him. This police action was initiated fo...

item.title: Scotland narrowly lose to Ivory Coast in final World Cup warm-up friendly
item.description: Scotland were narrowly defeated 1-0 by Ivory Coast in their final friendly before the World Cup, with Nicolas Pepe scoring the decisive goal after just 12 minutes. The match, held at Everton’s Hill Di...

item.title: Concerns over the legality of recent KMPDC appointments
item.description: The article examines recent controversies surrounding the Kenya Medical Practitioners and Dentists Council (KMPDC). It highlights allegations of bribery and misconduct within the Council, which have s...

item.title: Fort Hare VC Suspended Pending Disciplinary Investigation
item.description: University of Fort Hare’s vice-chancellor, Professor Sakhela Buhlungu, has been placed on precautionary suspension with full pay following a forensic investigation into irregularities in the appointme...

item.title: APC Elects New National Working Committee at Abuja Convention
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don finish their 8th national convention for Abuja wey confirm Prof Nentawe Yilwatda again as national chairman, while Sen. Ajibola Basiru still dey as national sec...

item.title: What the evidence says about medicinal cannabis for anxiety, depression and PTSD
item.description: Australians are increasingly using medicinal cannabis for common mental health conditions such as anxiety, depression and post‑traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), often with products that have high leve...

item.title: US-Israel Conflict with Iran Stalemates as Diplomacy Struggles to Gain Ground
item.description: On the thirty-second day of the escalating US-Israel war against Iran, the situation has hardened into a war of attrition with no decisive progress on either side. The United States and Israel have in...
